title: Rocky road – my sweet tooth gets the upper hand

seo.title: Rocky road - my sweet tooth gets the upper hand - Marinda Kook

html:
I don't know why my sweet tooth acts up like this. Rocky Road actually has no set quantities. You can make it just as your heart desires. It is and stays delicious. I used chocolate chunks that I bought from a bakery shop. You can use white or brown chocolate.

 

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 1 kg chocolate
  • 1 packet marshmallows
  • 1 packet biscuits – I used Romany Creams
  • 30 ml butter
  • 60 ml golden syrup

NOW:

  1. place a glass bowl over a pot full of water. The bowl must not touch the water. Switch the plate on and let the water simmer gently.
  2. place the chocolate in your glass bowl and let it melt
  3. stir in the butter and golden syrup
  4. crush the biscuits – not into crumbs, but into nice chunky pieces
  5. cut the marshmallows into quarters – if you're struggling, dip the knife in warm water
  6. take the chocolate off the pot and stir in the biscuits and marshmallows
  7. pour it into a dish lined with baking paper
  8. place it in the fridge to firm up
  9. cut it into blocks and ooh la la!

It's a lovely sweet treat for the lunchbox, for dessert, or just for a midnight snack.
